name: Salmon Souffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the canned salmon and reserve the juice. Flake the fish and set aside.
2. Line two 33 oz (1/2 pint) straight-sided bowls with cling film.
3. Blend the salmon, egg yolk, breadcrumbs, cheese, cream, smoked salmon, and horseradish sauce until smooth.
4. Heat the reserved salmon juice until almost boiling. Sprinkle in the gelatine and stir until dissolved. Mix into the salmon mixture.
5. Whisk the egg whites until stiff. Fold the salmon into the egg whites. Mix well. Divide between the two bowls.
6. Chill in a refrigerator until set.
7. Invert the bowls onto serving plates. Remove the bowls and cling film.
8. Press chopped watercress leaves around the sides of each souffle.
9. Cut the remaining smoked salmon into thin strips and arrange in a lattice across the tops.
10. Serve with mixed salad leaves, apple slices, and walnut pieces.
Serves 2. Approx. 585 kcals per serving
